steps.text: Calibrate, maintain, and operate the CEMS.
Conduct reference method tests to determine conformance of the CEMS with the specification.
Conduct calibration drift tests to determine conformance of the CEMS with the specification.
Conduct CEMS performance evaluations at other times besides the initial test.

preconditions.text: The Administrator requires CEMS performance evaluations at other times besides the initial test under Section 114 of the Act.

constraints.text: Diluent (O2) monitor must meet performance specifications in 40 CFR part 60, Appendix B, Performance Specification 3.
CEMS must be capable of measuring the total concentrations of two or more specified metals in both their vapor and solid forms.
If a CEMS does not measure a particular metal or fails to meet the performance specifications for a particular metal, it may not be used to determine emission compliance for that metal.

steps.text: Install the CEMS at an accessible location downstream of all pollution control equipment where the metals concentrations measurements are directly representative or can be corrected so as to be representative of the total emissions from the affected facility.
Select representative measurement points or paths for monitoring in locations that the CEMS will pass the RA test.
Select traverse points to determine the representativeness of the measurement location according to 40 CFR part 60, Appendix A, Method 1.
Relocate the CEMS.

preconditions.text: The cause of failure to meet the RA test is determined to be the measurement location and a satisfactory correction technique cannot be established, and the Administrator requires relocation.

constraints.text: The measurement location should be (1) at least eight equivalent diameters downstream of the nearest control device, point of pollutant generation, bend, or other point at which a change of pollutant concentration or flow disturbance may occur and (2) at least two equivalent diameters upstream from the effluent exhaust.
The measurement point should be (1) no less than 1.0 meter from the stack or duct wall or (2) within or centrally located over the centroidal area of the stack or duct cross section.

steps.text: Calculate the equivalent duct diameter as per 40 CFR part 60, Appendix A, Method 1, Section 2.1.
Select traverse measurement point locations according to 40 CFR part 60, Appendix A, Method 1, Sections 2.2 and 2.3.
Displace the RM traverse line up to 30 cm (or 5 percent of the equivalent diameter of the cross section, whichever is less) from the centroidal area.

preconditions.text: The RM traverse line interferes with or is interfered by the CEMS measurements

constraints.text: The effective measurement path of Path CEMS should be (1) totally within the inner area bounded by a line 1.0 meter from the stack or duct wall, or (2) have at least 70 percent of the path within the inner 50 percent of the stack or duct cross sectional area, or (3) be centrally located over any part of the centroidal area.

The RM measurement location should be (1) at least eight equivalent diameters downstream of the nearest control device, point of pollutant generation, bend, or other point at which a change of pollutant concentration or flow disturbance may occur and (2) at least two equivalent diameters upstream from the effluent exhaust.
The RM and CEMS locations should be as close as possible without causing interference with one another.

steps.text: Perform calibration at a point equal to 80 to 120 percent of the applicable emission standard for each metal.
Sample the stack effluent continuously.

constraints.text: The CEMS data recorder response range must include zero and a high level value equal to the span value (or have multiple outputs/auto-changing capability if a lower high level value is used).

The RA of the CEMS must be no greater than 20 percent of the mean value of the RM test data in terms of units of the emission standard for each metal, or 10 percent of the applicable standard, whichever is greater.
The CEMS calibration may not drift or deviate from the reference value (RV) of the calibration standard used for each metal by more than 5 percent of the emission standard for each metal.
The CEMS zero point for each metal shall not drift by more than 5 percent of the emission standard for that metal.
Averaging time, the number of measurements in an average, and the averaging procedure for reporting and determining compliance shall conform with that specified in the applicable emission regulation.
The response time for the CEMS must not exceed 2 minutes to achieve 95 percent of the final stable value.

steps.text: Perform sampling for batch CEMS.
Change the sample collection media.
Report the sample analysis.

constraints.text: Sampling time must be no longer than one third of the averaging period for the applicable standard.
The delay between the end of the sampling period and reporting of the sample analysis shall be no greater than one hour.
Sampling must be continuous except for pauses when changing media, which must be no greater than five percent of the averaging period or five minutes, whichever is less.

steps.text: Install the CEMS and prepare the RM test site according to the specifications in Section 3.
Prepare the CEMS for operation according to the manufacturer’s written instructions.
Determine the magnitude of the calibration drift (CD) and zero drift (ZD) once each day (at 24-hour intervals) for 7 consecutive days according to the procedure given in Section 6.
Conduct a RA test according to the procedure given in Section 7.
Conduct the CD test immediately before periodic automatic or manual adjustments, or in such a way that CD and ZD can be determined.
Conduct the CD and ZD tests at the points specified in Sections 4.3 and 4.4.
Record the CEMS response and calculate the CD.
Calculate the ZD.

preconditions.text: The affected facility is operating at more than 50 percent of normal load, or as specified in an applicable subpart.
No unscheduled maintenance, repair, or manual adjustment of the CEMS took place during the preceding 24-hour period.
Periodic automatic or manual adjustments are made to the CEMS zero and calibration settings.

constraints.text: None of the CD's or ZD's may exceed the specification.

steps.text: Spike the appropriate metals in the feed or otherwise operate the facility to raise the metal concentration to a level detectable by both the CEMS and the RM.

preconditions.text: Metals emissions concentrations from the source are so low as to be undetectable by the CEMS operating in its normal mode.

steps.text: Calculate the arithmetic mean of a data set using Equation 3.
Calculate the arithmetic mean of the difference, d, of a data set, using Equation 3 and substituting d for x.
Calculate the standard deviation (SD) of a data set using Equation 5.
Calculate the Relative Accuracy (RA) using Equation 6.
Summarize in tabular form the results of the CD tests and the RA tests or alternate RA procedure as appropriate.
Include all data sheets, calculations, and records of CEMS response necessary to substantiate that the performance of the CEMS met the performance specifications.
Report the CEMS measurements to the agency.

preconditions.text: Check with the appropriate regional office, or State, or local agency for additional requirements, if any.

constraints.text: Use n equal to the number of individual values when determining t-values from Table 1.
The CEMS measurements shall be reported in units of µg/m3 on a dry basis, corrected to 20°C or 7 percent O2.
